_template: Shape of `.deep-skills/<effort>/06-Bug-Fix/round-N/root-causes.json` — written under --learn only (see references/learn-record.md). Annotations live in `_doc` keys here; real root-causes.json files omit every key starting with `_`. One record per cluster that closed with proof verdict `fixed`, appended incrementally as each cluster closes (same rhythm as fix-summary.md); unproven, regressed, and skipped clusters NEVER produce a record — cause data here is proof-truthed, not claimed. Display-only today: the machine reader (the Deep-Learn Distiller / Pattern Ledger) does not exist yet; humans read the same causes in fix-summary.md. Fields stay stable — this shape is the Ledger's future ingestion payload.

records._doc_cluster_id: The cluster's id as diagnosis confirmed it (post split/merge), matching this round's scope.json and fix-summary.md
records._doc_confirmed_cause: The diagnose+fix agent's confirmed root cause — one sentence with the causal site as path:line. Sourced from independent diagnosis, never copied from the review's recommendation.
records.confirmed_cause: Write path and runtime read path disagree: TrackService.saveTrack POSTs /api/tracks but no such route is registered — the runtime reader resolves /api/track-templates (apps/ui/src/app/tracks/track.service.ts:88)
records._doc_review_hypothesis_upheld: true | false — did the review's (or reporter's) cause-guess (the defects' recommendation_hypothesis, taken together) survive independent diagnosis? false when diagnosis rejected or materially corrected it. This is the overfitting signal the Pattern Ledger wants: across efforts it measures how often review-time cause-guesses hold up. null only when the cluster arrived with no hypothesis at all (a bare BF-* report) — nothing to uphold.
records._doc_taxonomy_class: One of the class slugs in the shared directives registry's taxonomy.md — the authoritative list (write-no-reader | deletion-parity | claim-vs-evidence | test-hygiene | reuse-dup at the time of this template; always read the live file). Never invent a slug; a confirmed cause that fits no listed class records null, with the gap noted in the cluster's fix-summary.md entry.
records._doc_fix: One-paragraph summary of the minimal fix — files changed (path:line) + what changed at the cause. The full human record (why minimal, what was deliberately not touched) lives in fix-summary.md.
records.fix: Registered the POST /api/tracks route and wired it to the existing save handler (apps/api/src/routes/tracks.routes.ts:12) — the reader now resolves the source the writer writes
records._doc_findings_resolved: The ids of every defect the proof verdict covered — CR-* for review findings, BF-* for synthesized defects. Exactly the ids whose statuses flipped accepted → fixed (BF-* ids have no status; they are listed all the same).
records._doc_proof: tier: chain-trace | red-green (red-green under --reproduce; a reported-unreproducible defect proven by fallback keeps the cluster tier it was actually proven at). verdict is always `fixed` here — only proven clusters emit records — but is carried explicitly so the Ledger never infers it.
records._doc_provenance: Where this record came from, self-contained so the Distiller can ingest records across efforts and rounds without the surrounding file: the effort name, the bug-fix round number, and `review` = the effort-relative path of the findings.json the resolved findings came from (null for standalone BF-* defects with no review).
